name: When the Frost Is on the Punkin
recipeInstructions:
1. When the frost is on the punkin and the fodder's in the shock,
2. And you hear the quack and gobble of the struttin' turkey-cock
3. And the clattering of the geese, and the clucking of the hens,
4. And the rooster's crow as he tiptoes on the fence;
5. O, it's then's the time a feller is feeling at his best,
6. With the rising sun to greet him from a night of peaceful rest,
7. As he leaves the house, bareheaded, and goes out to feed the stock.
8. There's something hearty about the atmosphere
9. When the heat of summer's over and the cooling fall is here--
10. Of course we miss the flowers, and the blossoms on the trees,
11. And the hum of the hummingbirds and buzzing of the bees;
12. But the air is so appetizing; and the landscape through the haze
13. Of a crisp and sunny morning of the early autumn days
14. Is a picture that no painter has the coloring to mock--
15. When the frost is on the punkin and the fodder's in the shock.
16. The husky, rusty rustle of the tassels of the corn,
17. And the rasping of the tangled leaves, as golden as the morn;
18. The stubble in the furrows--kind of lonely but still
19. Preaching sermons to us of the barns they growed to fill;
20. The straw stack in the meadow, and the reaper in the shed;
21. The horses in their stalls below--the clover overhead!--
22. O, it sets my heart a-clicking like the ticking of a clock,
23. When the frost is on the punkin and the fodder's in the shock!
24. Then your apples are gathered, and the ones you keep
25. Are poured around the cellar floor in red and yellow heaps;
26. And your cider-making's over, and your wife's folks are through
27. With their mincemeat, apple butter, sausages, too!
28. ...I don't know how to tell it--but if such a thing could be
29. As the Angels wantin' boardin', and they'd call around on me--
30. I'd want to accommodate them all the whole-indurin' flock,
31. When the frost is on the punkin and the fodder's in the shock!
